items.quote: Love begins by taking care of the closest ones - the ones at home.
items.author: Mother Teresa

items.title: MARCH: Multi-Agent Radiology Clinical Hierarchy for CT Report Generation
items.abstract: MARCH is a multi-agent framework for CT report generation that mimics the hierarchical structure of radiology departments. It employs specialized agents (Resident, Fellow, Attending) to perform iterative drafting, retrieval-augmented revision, and consensus-based verification to mitigate clinical hallucinations. This approach significantly improves both the clinical fidelity and linguistic accuracy of automated reports compared to monolithic models.
items.figure_caption: Overview of the MARCH framework. It consists of three main stages: 1) Initial Report Drafting, 2) Retrieval-Augmented Report Revision, and 3) Consensus-Driven Finalization.

items.title: UniEditBench: A Unified and Cost-Effective Benchmark for Image and Video Editing via Distilled MLLMs
items.abstract: UniEditBench introduces a unified and cost-effective benchmark for evaluating both image and video editing models using a shared protocol, addressing the fragmentation in current evaluation methods. Its core contribution is a structured taxonomy covering diverse operations (including challenging compositional tasks) and the use of a distilled, lightweight MLLM judge to provide scalable evaluations that better align with human preference than traditional automatic metrics.
items.figure_caption: Figure 1. Overview of the UniEditBench framework. (A) Dataset Composition & Detailed Taxonomy: Displays the structured task taxonomy and data distribution, comprising 633 image and 77 video samples across diverse editing operations (e.g., content modification, attribute transformation, and compositional reasoning tasks like counting and reordering). (B) Evaluation Pipeline with Expert Distillation: Our pipeline provides a source prompt, target prompt, and edited prompt for each sample pair to accommodate various editing paradigms. Additionally, the editing results, original images, and prompt information are input into our 4B/8B discriminative models, which are distilled from the Qwen3-VL-235B-A22B model, to obtain the final judgments. (C) Comprehensive Benchmark Results: Radar charts display the aggregated evaluation results of editing methods on UniEditBench, assessed by the distilled 4B and 8B reward models (top: image benchmark; bottom: video benchmark).

items.title: Mind's Eye: A Benchmark of Visual Abstraction, Transformation and Composition for Multimodal LLMs
items.abstract: This paper introduces "Mind's Eye," a novel benchmark designed to assess the visual cognitive and visuospatial reasoning capabilities of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) through eight tasks categorized by Abstraction, Relation, and Transformation. The core contribution is demonstrating a significant gap, where top MLLMs score below 50% accuracy compared to 80% for humans, revealing limitations in visual attention, internal manipulation, and concept abstraction.
items.figure_caption: Overview of the eight tasks in the proposed Mind’s Eye benchmark : Each panel shows an example image-question pair of the benchmark

items.title: Prototype-Grounded Concept Models for Verifiable Concept Alignment
items.abstract: This paper introduces Prototype-Grounded Concept Models (PGCMs) to address the lack of verifiability in Concept Bottleneck Models (CBMs). PGCMs ground abstract concepts in learned visual prototypes, providing explicit image-part evidence for concept activation. This grounding allows for direct inspection and targeted human intervention to correct concept misalignments, achieving comparable predictive performance to CBMs while significantly enhancing transparency and interpretability.
items.figure_caption: Comparison between standard neural networks, Concept Bottleneck Models (CBMs), and our Prototype-Grounded Concept Models (PGCMs). While CBMs explain predictions in terms of high-level concepts, they provide no way to verify what those concepts mean visually. PGCMs ground each concept in learned visual prototypes, making concept semantics explicit and inspectable.

items.title: Robust Multispectral Semantic Segmentation under Missing or Full Modalities via Structured Latent Projection
items.abstract: This paper introduces CBC-SLP, a novel method for robust multispectral semantic segmentation that handles both missing and full modalities. The core method employs a **Structured Latent Projection (SLP)** approach to explicitly preserve both modality-invariant and modality-specific information, avoiding the performance trade-offs seen in models that rely solely on shared representations. This design ensures high performance regardless of modality availability.
items.figure_caption: Multispectral optical (false RGB: infrared-red-green channels) image on the left and DEM height map on the right, both from the Hunan image set [ 17 ] . While the multispectral image highlights surface reflectance and land-cover differences, the DEM height map reveals the terrain structure, providing complementary information for scene interpretation.

items.title: Information Router for Mitigating Modality Dominance in Vision-Language Models
items.abstract: This paper introduces the **Multi-modal Information Router ($\text{MoIR}$)** to combat modality dominance in Vision-Language Models (VLMs). $\text{MoIR}$ addresses the limitation of attention-based methods by employing an **information-level fusion** strategy. It explicitly reduces information disparity *before* fusion by identifying less informative tokens and routing complementary information from a stronger modality, thereby balancing the input signals.

items.title: Do Vision-Language Models Truly Perform Vision Reasoning? A Rigorous Study of the Modality Gap
items.abstract: This paper rigorously investigates whether Vision-Language Models (VLMs) perform genuine vision-grounded reasoning or rely on their textual components. The core method introduces **CrossMath**, a novel benchmark where problems are presented in text-only, image-only, and combined formats with strictly identical information content. The contribution is the finding that VLMs exhibit a **substantial performance gap**, consistently excelling on text-only inputs compared to image-based reasoning, suggesting limitations in their visual grounding.

items.description: FinceptTerminal is a modern finance application offering advanced market analytics, investment research, and economic data tools, designed for interactive exploration and data-driven decision-making in a user-friendly environment.
items.summary: FinceptTerminal is a modern finance application providing advanced market analytics, investment research, and economic data tools for interactive exploration and data-driven decision-making.

items.description: π RuView: WiFi DensePose turns commodity WiFi signals into real-time human pose estimation, vital sign monitoring, and presence detection — all without a single pixel of video.
items.summary: RuView is a system that leverages commodity WiFi signals to perform real-time human pose estimation, vital sign monitoring, and presence detection without using any video data.

items.description: AI You Control: Choose your models. Own your data. Eliminate vendor lock-in.
items.summary: Thunderbolt is an AI platform enabling users to control their models, own their data, and avoid vendor lock-in by offering flexible model choices.

items.description: A community-supported supercharged document management system: scan, index and archive all your documents
items.summary: Paperless-ngx is a community-supported document management system that allows users to scan, index, and archive all their documents efficiently.

items.description: Enterprise Architecture Governance & Vendor Procurement Toolkit
items.summary: Arc-Kit is an enterprise architecture governance and vendor procurement toolkit providing structured processes and resources for managing architectural standards and acquiring necessary technologies.

items.description: Real-time global intelligence dashboard. AI-powered news aggregation, geopolitical monitoring, and infrastructure tracking in a unified situational awareness interface
items.summary: WorldMonitor is a real-time global intelligence dashboard that uses AI to aggregate news, monitor geopolitics, and track infrastructure within a unified situational awareness interface.

items.description: A lightweight, powerful framework for multi-agent workflows
items.summary: The `openai-agents-python` repository provides a lightweight, powerful framework for building and managing complex multi-agent workflows using Python.

items.description: DeepGEMM: clean and efficient FP8 GEMM kernels with fine-grained scaling
items.summary: DeepGEMM provides clean and efficient FP8 General Matrix Multiplication (GEMM) kernels featuring fine-grained scaling for optimized performance.

items.description: A black hole for Internet advertisements
items.summary: Pi-hole is a network-level advertisement and tracking blocker that functions as a DNS sinkhole, protecting all devices on your network.
